stem: A right circular cylinder has a volume of 240 cubic centimeters. The area of the base of the cylinder is 15 square centimeters. What is the height, in centimeters, of the cylinder?
rationale: Choice C is correct. The volume V of a right circular cylinder equals the area of the base B times the height h: V = Bh. Substituting V = 240 and B = 15 yields 240 = 15h, so h = 240/15 = 16 centimeters. Choice A is the area of the base, not the height. Choice B is the base area squared. Choice D is the product of the area of the base and the volume.
